claim: Dynamic workflows spin up multiple sub-agents to run tasks in parallel, then synthesize results—different from /goal which loops until a criteria is met
actionable: Use workflows for width (many agents doing different things in parallel), use /goal for depth (one/few agents looping until objective met).
source_title: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows Clearly Explained

claim: Each agent in a workflow is a full Claude call with its own context window; cost scales with number of agents and context size—can easily consume 5M+ input tokens
actionable: Be very cautious with workflow scope—bound the task description, use Haiku for sub-agents where possible, avoid vague requests that spawn unnecessary agents.
source_title: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows Clearly Explained

claim: Workflows don't break into default global storage—manually configure to save in project .claude/workflows directory to keep them with your codebase
actionable: After creating a workflow, explicitly tell Claude Code to save it in your project's .claude/workflows folder—otherwise it defaults to a global location.
source_title: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows Clearly Explained

claim: Ultra code mode uses X-high effort reasoning with workflows as default for every prompt—very expensive but most capable
actionable: Avoid ultra code mode unless you need the most sophisticated reasoning—it defaults to workflows on every prompt, which is costly.
source_title: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows Clearly Explained

claim: Workflows are best for breaking problems into pieces that can run individually in parallel; avoid them for simple edits, quick questions, or general knowledge work
actionable: Ask: 'Does this break into many pieces that run independently in parallel?' If yes, use workflows; if no, use skills, sub-agents, or direct Claude Code.
source_title: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows Clearly Explained

claim: /deep-research automatically invokes workflows with parallel agents voting on claims, producing cited research reports
actionable: Use /deep-research for research tasks—it automatically orchestrates parallel agents with claim verification and citation, without manual workflow setup.
source_title: Claude Code Dynamic Workflows Clearly Explained
